title: Aureline history-edit recovery-object vocabulary
description: Boundary schema for the recovery_object_record family frozen in docs/vcs/history_edit_and_recovery_contract.md. One recovery_object_record pins one pre-mutation recovery payload (recovery_object_payload_class drawn from the closed six-value vocabulary covering local-history checkpoint, backup branch / tag ref, reflog anchor, export patch packet, safe worktree clone, and combined recovery packet) plus the upstream recovery_object_class re-exported from schemas/vcs/change_object.schema.json so every change-object / patch-stack / sequence-editor row that cites a recovery_object_ref resolves through one row from this family. One recovery_object_audit_event_record carries the closed denial-reason and event-id vocabularies. The record is the upstream every change-object, patch-stack, sequence-editor, history-edit-review, and sequence-step row reads through history_edit_recovery_record_id_ref before any topology-changing mutation is admitted; auto-continue is structurally blocked while the recovery object's lifecycle is anything other than recovery_object_minted_pre_mutation_pinned. Raw absolute paths, raw branch / commit URLs, raw author identity strings, raw commit message bodies, raw patch bodies, raw run-log bodies, raw approval-ticket bodies, raw notebook cell text, raw terminal bytes, and raw URLs MUST NOT appear on any record emitted against this schema; the record carries opaque ids, typed vocabulary, redaction-aware reviewable labels (<= 1024 graphemes), and policy / lifecycle state only. The eventual git-service crate's Rust types are the schema of record; this file is the cross-tool boundary every non-owning surface reads. Adding a new record kind, recovery_object_payload_class, recovery_object_lifecycle_state, recovery_object_topology_change_class, denial reason, or audit-event id is additive-minor and bumps recovery_object_schema_version; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

recovery_object_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for the recovery-object vocabulary. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape. Every record carries exactly one kind.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / journal entries / support bundles. Raw absolute paths, raw branch / commit URLs, raw author identity strings, raw commit message bodies, raw patch bodies, raw run-log bodies, raw approval-ticket bodies, raw notebook cell text, raw terminal bytes, and raw URLs never appear here.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

reviewable_label.description: Redaction-aware reviewable label (<= 1024 graphemes). Carries a presentation-only summary for support / audit; never carries the raw payload.

policy_context.description: Policy context the record was minted under. ADR-0008 / ADR-0009 policy epoch and ADR-0001 / ADR-0018 trust state are re-validated on every restore and topology-changing mutation.

recovery_object_class.description: Re-exported verbatim from schemas/vcs/change_object.schema.json. Names which upstream class of recovery object the change-object / patch-stack / sequence-editor row cites; the per-row payload class on this schema (recovery_object_payload_class) is the concrete payload pinned to the row, and the schema's allOf gates pair the two so a row cannot claim a class it cannot back.

recovery_object_payload_class.description: Frozen recovery-object payload vocabulary. local_history_checkpoint pins a local-history entry (schemas/recovery/local_history_entry.schema.json). backup_branch_or_tag_ref pins a named backup branch or tag ref the user can switch back to. reflog_anchor pins a reflog-style anchor at the pre-mutation HEAD / branch tip. export_patch_bundle pins an exported patch bundle the user can re-import. safe_worktree_clone pins a fresh worktree clone the user can fall back to. combined_recovery_packet cites two or more of the above through combined_recovery_packet_member_refs; used when a topology change spans multiple recovery surfaces (e.g. a sequence-editor plan that rewrites a patch stack pins both a reflog anchor and a patch-stack snapshot).

recovery_object_lifecycle_state.description: Frozen recovery-object lifecycle vocabulary. recovery_object_minted_pre_mutation_pinned is the state every topology-changing flow cites before mutation; auto-continue is admissible only when at least one cited recovery object is in this state. recovery_object_consumed_rolled_back is set after the user invoked rollback_to_pre_mutation_recovery_object; the row remains as a tombstone for audit. recovery_object_superseded_by_newer_recovery_object is set when a successor recovery object is minted (e.g. a multi-step sequence-editor plan refreshes the recovery object between steps); the predecessor cites the successor through superseded_by_recovery_object_id_ref. recovery_object_archived_tombstone is set when the row is archived after retention expiry.

recovery_object_topology_change_class.description: Frozen vocabulary describing which class of topology-changing operation pinned this recovery object. topology_change_history_rewrite covers interactive rebase / reword / squash / fixup / drop / edit. topology_change_revert_commit_append covers a non-rewriting revert that appends a new commit. topology_change_replay_onto_new_target covers cherry-pick / replay onto an alternate target. topology_change_patch_series_restack covers stack-wide restack onto a new base. topology_change_orphan_worktree_export covers an export-before-archive on an orphan worktree. topology_change_branch_label_move covers explicit branch-label moves the user requested with an anchor.

recovery_object_denial_reason.description: Frozen denial-reason vocabulary on the recovery_object audit stream.

recovery_object_audit_event_id.description: Frozen audit-event ids on the recovery_object stream. recovery_object_audit_denial_emitted MUST cite a recovery_object_denial_reason.

recovery_object_id.description: Opaque id naming this recovery-object row across change-object / patch-stack / sequence-editor / history-edit-review / sequence-step / support-export / audit lanes. Cited through history_edit_recovery_record_id_ref on every record family that reserved the slot.

workspace_id_ref.description: Opaque ref to the workspace row in schemas/workspace/* the recovery object was minted in.

target_branch_or_worktree_ref.description: Opaque ref to the branch or worktree the recovery object pins. A combined_recovery_packet that spans several worktrees pins the primary worktree here and cites the others through combined_recovery_packet_member_refs.

pre_mutation_revision_id_ref.description: Opaque ref to the pre-mutation revision the recovery object anchors. Always non-null; the anchor is the row a rollback resolves to.

local_history_entry_ref.description: Opaque ref into schemas/recovery/local_history_entry.schema.json. Required (non-null) when recovery_object_payload_class is local_history_checkpoint; null otherwise.

backup_branch_or_tag_ref.description: Opaque ref to a named backup branch / tag the user can switch back to. Required (non-null) when recovery_object_payload_class is backup_branch_or_tag_ref; null otherwise.

reflog_anchor_ref.description: Opaque ref to a reflog-style anchor pinning the pre-mutation HEAD / branch tip. Required (non-null) when recovery_object_payload_class is reflog_anchor; null otherwise.

export_patch_bundle_ref.description: Opaque ref to the exported patch bundle the user can re-import. Required (non-null) when recovery_object_payload_class is export_patch_bundle; null otherwise.

safe_worktree_clone_ref.description: Opaque ref to the safe worktree clone the user can fall back to. Required (non-null) when recovery_object_payload_class is safe_worktree_clone; null otherwise.

combined_recovery_packet_member_refs.description: Opaque refs to two or more sibling recovery_object_record rows that this combined packet cites. Required (length >= 2) when recovery_object_payload_class is combined_recovery_packet; empty otherwise (denies with combined_recovery_packet_member_refs_forbidden_for_non_combined_class on a non-empty list when the payload class is not combined_recovery_packet).

superseded_by_recovery_object_id_ref.description: Opaque ref to the successor recovery_object_record row that supersedes this one. Required (non-null) when recovery_object_lifecycle_state is recovery_object_superseded_by_newer_recovery_object; null otherwise.

command_id_ref.description: Opaque ref to the command-dispatch id under which the recovery object was minted. Nullable on system-driven mints (e.g. an automatic checkpoint pre-rebase); required (non-null) on user-driven mints.

approval_ticket_ref.description: Opaque ref to the approval-ticket row admitting the topology-changing mutation this recovery object pins. Nullable until the mutation is admitted; required (non-null) once the recovery object has been pinned to a confirmed topology-changing operation.

consumed_at.description: Required (non-null) when recovery_object_lifecycle_state is recovery_object_consumed_rolled_back. Null otherwise.

archived_at.description: Required (non-null) when recovery_object_lifecycle_state is recovery_object_archived_tombstone. Null otherwise.

allOf.description: local_history_checkpoint payload MUST cite a non-null local_history_entry_ref and pair with pre_mutation_local_history_checkpoint_minted recovery_object_class.
backup_branch_or_tag_ref payload MUST cite a non-null backup_branch_or_tag_ref and pair with pre_mutation_branch_label_anchor_minted recovery_object_class.
reflog_anchor payload MUST cite a non-null reflog_anchor_ref and pair with pre_mutation_reflog_anchor_minted recovery_object_class.
export_patch_bundle payload MUST cite a non-null export_patch_bundle_ref and pair with pre_mutation_patch_stack_snapshot_minted recovery_object_class.

safe_worktree_clone payload MUST cite a non-null safe_worktree_clone_ref and pair with pre_mutation_combined_recovery_packet_minted recovery_object_class so the worktree clone is admitted only as part of a combined recovery surface (the worktree alone is admissible upstream when combined_recovery_packet_member_refs cite this row).
combined_recovery_packet payload MUST cite at least two combined_recovery_packet_member_refs and pair with pre_mutation_combined_recovery_packet_minted recovery_object_class.
Non-combined payload classes MUST cite an empty combined_recovery_packet_member_refs list (denies with combined_recovery_packet_member_refs_forbidden_for_non_combined_class on a non-empty list).
recovery_object_consumed_rolled_back MUST cite a non-null consumed_at.
recovery_object_archived_tombstone MUST cite a non-null archived_at.

recovery_object_record.description: Row shape consumed by the change-stack panel, the source-control panel, the patch-stack panel, the sequence-editor plan view, the history-edit review surface, the per-step sequence-step row, the support / export bundle, and the audit lane. Exactly one row per recovery-object identity; the row is the upstream every history_edit_recovery_record_id_ref slot resolves through.

denial_reason.description: Required (non-null) when recovery_object_audit_event_id_value is recovery_object_audit_denial_emitted. Null otherwise.

allOf.description: denial_reason is required (non-null) when the event id is recovery_object_audit_denial_emitted and MUST be null otherwise.

recovery_object_audit_event_record.description: Audit-event row on the recovery_object stream.
